supplies.title: Household Items - General Science: Grade 5
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
• honey
• food items suggested
• spoons
• small saucepan
• plant fertilizer of your choice (only a small amount)
• water
• flowers (Term 1)
• a strainer or sieve
• 1 c dry soil
• kitchen scale
• kitchen thermometer, any stick-type tool that can be placed into hot water and soil
• oven
• oven mitt
• clock
• chair or stepladder
• stopwatch, such as found on most electronic devices

• stove
• 1-2 c white vinegar
• 1/2-1 c baking soda

supplies.title: Household Items - Science: Chemistry
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
• 3 small objects
• distilled or deionized water 
• mineral spirits/paint thinner (may substitute mineral oil or cooking oil, but won't be able to evaporate the solvent at the end of the lab)
• 4 recycled soda cans
• sticky tack, a piece of foam, or anything to hold thermometer in soda can and close the opening
• hydrogen peroxide antiseptic
• barometer or computer access to check local weather station
• ice
• sugar
• salt
• spoon
• lemon juice
• stopwatch, such as those found on most electronic devices
• microwave-safe bowl 
• microwave
• whisk that you do not care about
• another whisk or electric mixer
• well-ventilated space (porch, carport, etc)
• juice or tea
• quart-sized mason jar with a lid
• jar, cup, or bowl
• slotted spoon, strainer, or coffee filter
• 2 large egg whites (Term 3)
• large mixing bowl
• cookie sheet
• oven
• optional: hard blunt tip (knitting needle, skewer, etc)
• optional: 227g coconut oil from the grocery store, IF you want to use the soap you will make
• optional: lemon-lime soda, ice cream, yogurt, or cottage cheese (Term 3)
• optional: cream of tartar
• optional: piping bag
• optional: food coloring
• optional: parchment paper

supplies.title: Organic Chemistry Model Kit
supplies.rationale: Helps students accurately visualize the three-dimensional structure of simple molecules.
supplies.maySub: any molecule modeling kit.  Marshmallows and toothpicks can also be used, but are not self-correcting like the kits.

supplies.title: Food-Grade Calcium Chloride
supplies.rationale: The calcium chloride supplied in lab kits is not food-grade and NOT for consumption.  If students would like to eat the dessert caviar they make, they should obtain a food-grade chemical.
supplies.qty: 1 for 1-4 students (possibly more depending on interest)

supplies.title: Food-Grade Sodium Alginate
supplies.rationale: The sodium alginate supplied in lab kits is not food-grade and NOT for consumption.  If students would like to eat the dessert caviar they make, they should obtain a food-grade chemical.
supplies.qty: 1 for 1-4 students (possibly more depending on interest)

supplies.title: Food-Grade Sodium Hydroxide
supplies.rationale: The sodium hydroxide supplied in lab kits is not food-grade and is not for skin contact use.  If students would like to produce soap for use, then a food-grade chemical should be purchased.

books.title: Alveary High School Biology Kit
books.rationale: Supplies harder-to-find materials for labs and lessons.  General safety information is provided by manufacturer(s) and in the lab books, but teachers should educate themselves regarding local regulations for safe disposal of any materials, especially lab or industrial chemicals.
books.note:
There is a shortage on frog dissection specimens.  HST has a limited number of Alveary kits that include this item.  Once those kits are gone, they will adjust the price.  This specimen is not needed to get started, but teachers will need to obtain this item separately if not included.

Canadian members: The Learning House is able to provide the HST kits in Canada.
books.maySub: comparable items, if purchased or found separately; refer to kit contents at HST website for a list of supplies included in the kits and/or lessons/lab books for a comprehensive list of supplies needed for each lab.
books.qty: 1 kit for 1-3 students (Could stretch to 4, if needed.)

books.title: Elodea
books.note: Elodea is invasive in some areas and cannot be disposed of outside; it must be discarded in the trash or used in an aquarium when finished.
books.maySub: another water plant with many thin leaves.

books.title: Household Items - Science: Biology
books.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
• any living plant specimen found nearby
• any non-living specimen 
• red onion (Term 1)
• kitchen knife
• distilled or deionized water
• paper towels
• lamp or bright light
• strawberries (Term 1)
• salt
• a variety of colors of small Lego bricks
• computer access
• stopwatch, such as those found on most electronic devices
• cereal O's 
• small bread cubes or mini marshmallows
• chopsticks
• fork

books.title: Frog Dissection Specimen
books.rationale: High School biology students complete a frog dissection as part of a series to learn about introductory anatomy and physiology.
books.note: Due to a shortage, a limited number of Alveary Biology kits will include this item.  IF you need to purchase this item separately, teachers will need to obtain this item for Term 3.
books.maySub: any preserved frog specimen
